RESPONDENT FILED A NOTICE OF NON-OPPOSITION. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998
SUMMONS ISSUED TO PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY
MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN OPPOSITION TO PETITION TO VACATE AND/OR CORRECT ARBITRATION AWARD FILED BY RESPONDENT SEIU LOCAL 790-DONISE MANCHESTER

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY'S MOTION TO VACATE AND/OR CORRECT ARBITRATION AWARD IS GRANTED. THE AWARD IS VACATED. THE PARTIES ARE TO MEET AND CONFER IN GOOD FAITH TO SEEK ALTERNATIVES TO RESPONDENT'S LAYOFF. THE AWARD IS CONTRARY TO ARTICLE 9 OF THE MOU AND AS A CONSEQUENCE, THE REMEDY FASHIONED EXCEEDS THE POWER OF THE ARBITRATOR. (CCP 1286.2) ADVANCED MURO DEVICES, INC. V. INTEL CORP. (1994) 9 CAL. 4TH 362, 383; PACIFIC MOTOR TRUCKING CO. V. AUTOMOTIVE MACHINISTS (9TH CIR. 1983) 702F.2D 176; IBEW, LOCAL 175 V. THOMAS & BETTS CORP. (6TH CIR. 1999) 182F. 3D 469, 472. THE DECISION IN SOCIAL SERVICES UNION ET AL V. ALAMEDA COUNTY, ET AL (1989) 207 CAL. APP. 3D 1458 IS NOT CONTROLLING FOR THE REMEDY WAS CONSISTENT WITH THE MOU. THE ARBITRATOR FOUND THAT PETITIONER VIOLATED THE MEET AND CONFER PROVISIONS OF THE MOU AND THE APPROPRIATE REMEDY IS TO DIRECT THE PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER AND, IN GOOD FAITH, SEEK ALTERNATIVES TO RESPONDENTS LAYOFF. AT MR. HARRIGNTON'S REQUEST, THE COURT STAYED THE EFFECT OF THE ORDER TO SEPTEMBER 1, 2008. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK MAHONEY; REPORTER: HELEN SOREP-MATLEY, CSR 	225

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT SEIU LOCAL 790'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER DENYING PETITIONER'S MOTION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD AND CONFIRMING ARBITRATION AWARD IS GRANTED. THE PROPOSED ORDER INCLUDING THE REFERENCE TO COSTS IS PROPER. IF THE PREVAILING PARTY FILES A COSTS MEMORANDUM THAT INCLUDES IMPROPER OR UNTIMELY COSTS, THEN PETITIONERS SHOULD FILE A MOTION TO TAX TO CONTEST THE COSTS. SEE CRC 8.278(C)(2) AND CRC 3.1700(B). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT (COPY MAILED TO RESPONDENT). JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Off Calendar Oct-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
RESPONDENT SEIU LOCAL 790 MOTION FOR AN ORDER DENYING PETITIONER'S MOTION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD AND CONFIRMING ARBITRATION AWARD.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - GRANTED. THE PROPOSED ORDER INCLUDING THE REFERENCE TO COSTS IS PROPER. IF THE PREVAILING PARTY FILES A COSTS MEMORANDUM THAT INCLUDES IMPROPER OR UNTIMELY COSTS, THEN PETITIONERS SHOULD FILE A MOTION TO TAX TO CONTEST THE COSTS. SEE CRC 8.278(c)(2) AND CRC 3.1700(b). Order signed in open court (copy mailed to respondent). (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion To Vacate And/Or Correct Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Aug-01-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY'S MOTION TO VACATE AND/OR CORRECT ARBITRATION AWARD

Appearances:
Kevin Cholakian of Cholakian & Associates (467.8200) for petitioner and moving party
Vincent Harrington, Jr. of Weinberg Roger & Rosenfeld (510.337.1001) for respondent and opposing party

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: the award is vacated. The parties are to meet and confer in good faith to seek alternatives to respondent's layoff. The award is contrary to Article 9 of the MOU and as a consequence, the remedy fashioned exceeds the power of the arbitrator. (CCP 1286.2) Advanced Muro Devices, Inc. v. Intel Corp. (1994) 9 Cal. 4th 362, 383; Pacific Motor Trucking Co. v. Automotive Machinists (9th Cir. 1983) 702F.2d 176; IBEW, Local 175 v. Thomas & Betts Corp. (6th Cir. 1999) 182F. 3d 469, 472. The decision in Social Services Union et al v. Alameda County, et al (1989) 207 Cal. App. 3d 1458 is not controlling for the remedy was consistent with the MOU. The arbitrator found that petitioner violated the meet and confer provisions of the MOU and the appropriate remedy is to direct the parties to meet and confer and, in good faith, seek alternatives to respondents layoff. At Mr. Harrignton's request, the Court stayed the effect of the order to September 1, 2008. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley, CSR 225

Notice Of Motion To Vacate And/Or Correct Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Jul-18-2008 Continued To Aug-01-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY'S MOTION TO VACATE AND/OR CORRECT ARBITRATION AWARD

Appearances:
Richard Lin of Cholakian & Associates (467-8200) for petitioner and moving party

No other appearances on this matter. Mr. Lin appeared on behalf of all parties to request a different hearing date. The Court modified its ruling and continued the matter to August 1, 2008. Mr. Lin to give notice. Moving party to provide courtesy copies of its moving papers. (302/PJM) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
